2_faq_schema._description: FAQPage schema — helps capture FAQ rich results in Google

mainEntity.name: What CNC machining services does Mesquite Manufacturing offer?

acceptedAnswer.text: Mesquite Manufacturing offers precision CNC machining and milling, prototype development, custom assembly, and 3D printing services. We use fourth axis CNC technology and hold tolerances to ±0.001".
We are located in Mesquite, TX — in the Dallas–Fort Worth metroplex. We serve clients throughout Texas and nationally, with 24/7 availability.

mainEntity.name: What tolerances can Mesquite Manufacturing hold?

acceptedAnswer.text: Our CNC machining capabilities include tolerances to ±0.001" to ±0.005" depending on material, geometry, and complexity.
We machine a wide range of materials including aluminum alloys (6061, 7075), stainless steel, carbon steel, titanium, brass, and engineering plastics such as Delrin, PEEK, and nylon.

mainEntity.name: How do I get a quote for CNC machining?

acceptedAnswer.text: You can submit a Request for Quote (RFQ) directly on our website. Upload your CAD files, specify your material and tolerance requirements, and we'll respond within 24 hours.

4th axis CNC machining adds rotational movement to a standard 3-axis CNC machine. This allows us to machine complex geometries in fewer setups, reducing cost and improving accuracy for intricate parts.

mainEntity.name: Do you offer rush or expedited machining services?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. Mesquite Manufacturing operates 24/7 and can accommodate rush orders. Contact us directly to discuss expedited timelines and availability.

mainEntity.name: Do you provide prototype development services?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. We offer rapid CNC prototyping services with iterative design feedback. Our team helps you go from concept to production-ready part efficiently.
We serve aerospace, defense, oil & gas, automotive, medical device, electronics, and industrial equipment industries, among others.

mainEntity.name: What file formats do you accept for CNC machining quotes?

acceptedAnswer.text: We accept STEP (.stp), IGES (.igs), DXF, DWG, and PDF drawings. STEP files are preferred for 3D geometry. Contact us if you need assistance with file formats.
